CASE-09 Active Investigation

Eskom Procurement Fraud -- R21bn Diesel Contract

Opened 03 Jun 2026 - 04:48 · Updated 03 Jun 2026 - 04:48 · 19 Jun 2026 18:49 UTC
0.34
Evidence Confidence
6
Pinned Signals
2
Linked Actors
0.44
Peak Signal
Download this case brief as PDF
Case Summary
AmaBhungane (May 2026): Minenhle Mavuso (24, BA Psychology) operated front company for R21bn Eskom diesel supply contract (2024). CIPC: zero registrations for Mavuso or Milo Hair Beaute. HAWKS signals confirm systematic bogus-supplier fraud pattern. ABB state capture reparations (R2.5bn) establish institutional vulnerability.
Analyst Hypothesis
Mavuso is a leaf node. Authorizing official approved her supplier registration and contract award. Upstream chain leads to Eskom executive or political interference.
Evidence — 6 Signals
Inside Eskom’s dodgy R21-billion diesel contract
AmaBhungane has exposed a R21 billion Eskom diesel procurement contract linked to Minenhle Mavuso, a 24-year-old BA Psychology graduate who simultaneously operated an online hair products store. Mavuso is alleged to have...
AMABHUNGANE 10 May 2026 - 01:33 Significant

CIPC public records search returned zero registration entries for Minenhle Mavuso and zero for Milo Hair Beaute. An unregistered entity holding a R21-billion government procurement contract is a structural fraud indicato...

Methodology
This investigation comprises 6 open-source signals and 2 linked actors. Evidence confidence is derived from signal gravity scores (urgency, source credibility, recency, corroboration). Actor relationships are manually verified unless marked as automated extraction. All source material is publicly available. This brief is produced by the FORGE OSINT engine. Inclusion reflects analytical relevance and does not constitute a legal finding.
